2026-02-04T11:07:41.8876646Z pybind11 provides CMake targets. There are two modes provided; classic, which is built on the old Python
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8876965Z discovery packages in CMake, or the new FindPython mode, which uses FindPython
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8877174Z from 3.12+ forward (3.15+ _highly_ recommended).
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8877272Z 
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8877416Z New FindPython mode:
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8877500Z 
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8877654Z     find_package(Python COMPONENTS Interpreter Development)
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8877832Z     find_package(pybind11 CONFIG)
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8877928Z 
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8878054Z     # pybind11 method:
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8878223Z     pybind11_add_module(MyModule1 src1.cpp)
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8878322Z 
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8878441Z     # Python method:
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8878595Z     Python_add_library(MyModule2 src2.cpp)
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8878790Z     target_link_libraries(MyModule2 pybind11::headers)
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8878979Z     set_target_properties(MyModule2 PROPERTIES
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8879152Z         INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION ON
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8879311Z         CXX_VISIBILITY_PRESET ON
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8879475Z         VISIBILITY_INLINES_HIDDEN ON
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8879620Z     )
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8879702Z 
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8879830Z For more information see here:
2026-02-04T11:07:41.8880014Z     https://pybind11.readthedocs.io/en/latest/compiling.html#building-with-cmake
